title: issue-003 re-drive: pre-identity stamping throw is the update refusal's root cause; the blocked pipeline misattributes it as item-validation failure
question: Why did update refuse a conventions 1.0.0->1.0.1 forward-migration whose items satisfy the target schema by inspection, with dry-run predicting resynced while the live run refused (issue-003's filed residual: re-drive the reproduction with the shipped blockedDetail diagnostic and fix or retire on the evidence)?
method: empirical-test + code-inspection: two-cell scratch-substrate reproduction (conventions schema installed at 1.0.0 against the 1.0.1 catalog, 45 rules valid against 1.0.1 by construction, install baseline recorded) differing in exactly one variable — presence of config.substrate_id — driven through the real built updateContext dry + live per cell; follow-through resolveBlocked run on the blocked cell's post-block state; corroborating read of resyncSchema's catch and resolveBlocked's commit sequence.
scope: workflowsPiExtension main at f7d6f819 (pi-context 0.31.0 working tree)
findings_summary: The refusal is infrastructure, not validation. Cell A (no substrate_id): dry-run predicts migrated; the live run returns blocked with reason validation-failed whose synthetic failure (keyword 'error') carries the real message — substrateIdForDir: config.json has no valid substrate_id — thrown by writeBlockForDir's mandatory identity stamping inside resyncSchema's catch. Cell B (identical fixture with a substrate_id): live migrates cleanly. Single-variable isolation confirms issue-003's root cause is the pre-identity resync limitation already filed as FGAP-033 (DEC-0012's named deferred consequence); the conventions items were never invalid, and no validation defect exists. Dry-run diverges because simulateResyncOutcome models migration + AJV re-validation in memory only and never exercises write preconditions. The re-drive also surfaced an untracked class, filed as FGAP-115 (refusal-cause conflation): the catch classifies EVERY throw as validation-failed, so blockedDetail directs the operator to fix valid items; git-style failure markers are inscribed into the fully-valid block file with a pending-blocked record persisted; the routed remedy resolve-blocked re-validates (passes) then throws uncaught mid-commit on the same stamping precondition, leaving partial state (schema advanced to 1.0.1, markers stripped, block unwritten, pending entry stale) — a violation of the per-component byte-exact refusal discipline; and the closed FGAP-066 dry-predicts-precisely guarantee is silently violated for this class. Disposition enacted: issue-003 resolved with this evidence; FGAP-033 related as the mechanism; FGAP-115 filed for the conflation class; the pre-identity design question is resolved by derivation from project precedent (ceremonies heal lagging substrates — config-version seeding, envelope convergence, registry propagation — and ceremonies already mint substrate_id at init/accept-all) toward ceremony-entry identity establishment, to be enacted per DEC-0012's requirement of a dedicated DEC.
stale_conditions: resyncSchema's version-bump catch (index.ts:1242-1270) gains refusal-cause classification (FGAP-115's resolution) — the misattribution findings describe superseded behavior
a ceremony-entry identity-establishment decision lands (FGAP-033 resolution) — pre-identity substrates no longer reach the stamping throw via update
resolveBlocked's commit sequence gains all-or-nothing discipline — the partial-commit finding describes superseded behavior

citations.label: resolveBlocked commit sequence outside the validation try
substrateIdForDir loud guard (no lazy mint on write)
